key_insight: A thermodynamic, multiscale framework shows that hydrogen trapping around a straight screw dislocation in bcc iron is dominated by a few statistically relevant configurations, especially the easy-core state.
engineering_relevance: The results provide a physically grounded way to model hydrogen-dislocation interactions in iron, improving embrittlement and plasticity predictions for larger-scale constitutive simulations.
method: Molecular simulations with a neural-network interatomic potential
Systematic exploration of hydrogen binding across easy/hard dislocation cores, site types, and concentrations
Thermodynamic ranking of trapping configurations and comparison to an elastic dipole description
evidence_signal: The framework is validated against the limited density functional theory data available in the literature and used to rationalize experimental hydrogen solubility limits.
watch_item: The simplified elastic dipole model is only valid within a limited interaction regime and may miss core-specific behavior outside that domain.
paper_title: Hydrogen segregation around a straight screw dislocation in bcc iron
